item.title: Memes and trans rights.

item.dc:creator: SolidSyco

item.title: YAAAAYYY!!!!!! :D
item.description:
Re-establishing contact was announced five days ago on April 22nd, but I only just learned about it. I’m so happy! I was worried that we’d lost another beloved machine. I’m still sad about Cassini, Curiosity, and Pioneers 10 & 11. I’m sad that we lost contact with Hyugens as well, but it had a pretty hard landing when it hit Titan. There will be more goodbyes to come, but at least we still have Voyager 1 for a little bit longer. This makes me especially happy, because Voyager 1 is going to be the hardest goodbye for me. . Voyager 1 is my favorite. I love to go onto the NASA website and follow her journey using the fly-by. I like to pretend that I’m sitting on her long attena, traveling towards the next star. Whenever I feel sad, I can think about the marvels of the universe that exist outside of the pale blue dot that we call home. Voyager 1 has seen massive planets, and danced with them while using their gravitational pull to slingshot her way towards the other stars. She saw the little pale dot that is our planet fade away from view as she slipped further away into the unknown. She’s heard the sound of interstellar space (space has sound, it just can’t travel due to the lack of air). She sees the incomprehensible amount of stars in the world outside of our solar system. The sun no longer illuminates her features because she’s so far away, it looks like a firefly. Voyager 1 makes me happy, because she reminds me that there is an entire universe beyond our world that’s waiting to be explored. She tells us things we’ve never known. She’s a literally distant friend, but a cherished one. I know it’s silly to have an emotional attachment to a machine, but we all have silly things that are important to us. I’m going to be super sad the day that we truly have to say goodbye to Voyager 1. I’m overjoyed to know that day has not come yet!! . . For those that don’t know what I’m talking about…. . EDIT: Opportunity Rover, not Curiosity! My bad!
For five months, Voyager 1 kept sending back silent phone calls. Scientists realized that the trouble came from a corrupted computer chip. They found a way to rearrange the spacecraft’s coding to work around the faulty chip. I don’t know shit about code, so the specifics of what they did are lost on me. I included a link in my sources cited for those that are more tech savvy than me!
We're not fully back in business yet. There's still some issues to sort out with getting scientific data again. However, this engineering message is fantastic, because it means we CAN get Voyager 1 back!!!
This is one of the reasons that it’s taking a while to get back to business. Both Voyager spacecrafts are so far away from Earth, it takes roughly 24 hours to send a message to them, and 24 hours to receive a message from them. Obviously cell service isn’t fantastic in interstellar space lol.
Many of Voyager 1’s instruments have been turned off either to save power, or because of a degraded performance. I made a side by side picture of what still works, and what’s turned off. Everything in grey are instruments that are turned off.
This is The Pale Blue Dot that I was talking about. Our planet looks like a tiny star in another, more distant planet’s sky. We’re probably part of a constellation that has best and lowest visibility, depending on the weather and the planet’s rotation. We shine the brightest during one season, and weakly flicker in other seasons. We are a fleck of dust in comparison to the universe in its entirety, and it shows by the pale blue dot that is our home from billions of miles away. This always blows my mind!
The sun has a STRONG gravitational pull. In fact, it’s so powerful that no spacecraft could possibly escape it and explore the outer planets of the solar system. To get further into space, satellites use what’s called “gravity assist,” aka a “slingshot maneuver,” by getting close to Jupiter. Jupiter’s gravitational pull is strong enough that it increases their speed, basically launching it deeper into the solar system like a slingshot. It’s a useful maneuver, but it’s a smidge risky because you have to be very precise. If a spacecraft gets too close, it will be sucked into Jupiter’s orbit.
Voyager Image Source: Sun From Voyager 1: https://e3.365dm.com/17/12/1600x900/skynews-nasa-voyager_4171953.jpg?20171202083204 . . Sources Cited: Voyager 1’s Official Mission Website: https://voyager.jpl.nasa.gov/mission/status/ ↑ If you click on “View Voyager 1” it will link you to its flyby simulator! Success on Re-Establishing Contact: https://phys.org/news/2024-04-nasa-voyager-distant-spacecraft-earth.html NPR’s Article on Re-Establishing Contact: https://www.npr.org/2024/04/23/1246392066/nasa-voyager-1-spacecraft-talks-back Here’s How NASA Fixed the Code: https://www.usatoday.com/story/graphics/2024/04/24/how-nasa-solved-voyager-1-computer-problem/73424132007/ Gravitational Slingshot 1: https://www.scienceabc.com/innovation/gravitational-slingshot-how-did-gravity-assist-voyager-1-2-in-escaping-the-solar-system.html Gravitational Slingshot 2: https://thewire.in/the-sciences/gravity-assists-flybys-bepicolombo-parker-probe The Pale Blue Dot: https://science.nasa.gov/resource/voyager-1s-pale-blue-dot/ The Sound of Interstellar Space: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LIAZWb9_si4&list=PLyqRbJF5PWanL8O3WxEJ9iBSL1Thk11e1&index=1

item.title: A hexagonal storm with a diameter of 25,000 km raging at the north pole of Saturn
item.description: Saturn's hexagon is a persistent approximately hexagonal cloud pattern around the north pole of the planet Saturn, located at about 78°N. The sides of the hexagon are about 14,500 km (9,000 mi) long, which is about 2,000 km (1,200 mi) longer than the diameter of Earth The hexagon may be a bit more than 29,000 km (18,000 mi) wide, may be 300 km (190 mi) high, and may be a jet stream made of atmospheric gases moving at 320 km/h (200 mph). It rotates with a period of 10h 39m 24s, the same period as Saturn's radio emissions from its interior. The hexagon does not shift in longitude like other clouds in the visible atmosphere. Saturn's hexagon was discovered during the Voyager mission in 1981, and was later revisited by Cassini-Huygens in 2006. During the Cassini mission, the hexagon changed from a mostly blue color to more of a golden color. Saturn's south pole does not have a hexagon, as verified by Hubble observations. It does, however, have a vortex, and there is also a vortex inside the northern hexagon.[13] Multiple hypotheses for the hexagonal cloud pattern have been developed.
item.dc:creator: ddpaladiya

item.title: Laney Boggs - She's All That - Cosplay
item.description:
I have been told for years I look like Rachael Leigh Cook in this movie, so I caved and finally did the cosplay :P So here is me as Laney Boggs "The Nerd" and me as Laney Boggs the "Hot Chick".
Main references for this cosplay :D
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: Tesla vs Unions
item.description: Just a friendly reminder that the conflict with Tesla in Sweden is still ongoing! :) Strikes and blockades since October - every now and then we get added measures like this company announcing they will no longer clear vehicle lifts for use (which can cause cascade problems if they potentially break the law by still using them etc). Unionize, we are stronger together! Original article at https://www.gp.se/ekonomi/unionen-varslar-om-blockad-vill-oka-trycket-pa-tesla.832bcc60-834d-4caf-b414-104cf0e6708e , just ran it through google translate.

item.title: HELP ME GET MY CAT DOWN

Keep scrolling for cat tax
This is my cat Paste (aka Lil G), and oh what's that next to him why it's one of my top-quality stickers I didn't even realize was in the photo until right now. Anyway here's a link to my shop I got a sale going on https://shop.loadingartist.com

item.title: Sometimes, you get what you pay for
item.dc:creator: Fanner50

item.title: Update on my life saving attempts
item.description: Update from original post: https://imgur.com/gallery/HIDLDjQ The rider survived. He lost his leg, which does not surprise me based on the scene. He also had two punctured lungs. But he is stable and still here. I am so glad I was able to help even if it wasn't a perfect outcome, life is so precious. The PTSD is real though my dudes. The moment I stopped moving today I feel like my insides imploded. I'm now in such a tizzy I'm not really speaking out loud. I just want to deep dive into a game or two. I'm gonna seek help and take it easy, but I appreciate this community and all your words and stories. If there's anyone who has been exposed to the bloody horrors of mortality, I would love to decompress in a way that doesn't make my husband vomit (bless him, but that leg was not a leg when I got there, and that is a bit much for most) so please message me if you dont mind a reply that might not come for a few days. Thanks imgurians, stay wonderful x
item.dc:creator: FluroShoes

item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Guess what caused the sink to back up
item.description:
Our sinks are back to back, or butt to butt, or whatever you want to picture. My jerkass neighbour puts all manner of ill shit down his stupid garburator, usually between 11pm-1am, because he's a jerkass. Sometimes I think he runs it for fun. We put nothing down our sink that isn't water because we're not dipshits, our rent is crazy cheap and it's an old as fuck building. The first time something went wrong with our sink four years ago management asked if they could take out the garburator and we said yes because they just cause problems. Anyway, our sink started fucking up and this nice kid of a plumber and an apprentice show up, figure out what the problem is, fix all the stuff, had to brave the stink of the jerkass neighbour's place (he's a gross old man and I'd feel sorry for him if he wasn't such a fucking asshole who talks to his dog in a really mean way), assess the problem on his side and they're going to recommend that his garburator be removed. Huzzah. I even had a bunch of booties laying around from a show my husband wasn't working on anymore and when they mentioned they were out, I was able to hook them up so that was nice.
My handsome friend was not impressed with all of the noise but he got treats out of it so he was happy. Edited to add: a garburator is a garbage disposal in Canadian, booties go on your feet inside someone's house to keep the floor clean, my husband had some from working on a tv show, not with seafood. Thank you for coming to my I Hate My Neighbour Ted Talk, I'm recovering from surgery and my husband is away for work and things are just a bit bleak so this was fun, probably.
item.dc:creator: ImmortalChild
